1	-- EMACS settings: -*-  tab-width: 2; indent-tabs-mode: t -*-
2	-- vim: tabstop=2:shiftwidth=2:noexpandtab
3	-- kate: tab-width 2; replace-tabs off; indent-width 2;
4	-- =============================================================================
5	-- Authors:         Martin Zabel
6	--
7	-- Entity:          Cache with cache controller to be used within a CPU
8	--
9	-- Description:
10	-- -------------------------------------
11	-- This unit provides a cache (:ref:`IP:cache_par2`) together
12	-- with a cache controller which reads / writes cache lines from / to memory.
13	-- The memory is accessed using a :ref:`INT:PoC.Mem` interfaces, the related
14	-- ports and parameters are prefixed with ``mem_``.
15	--
16	-- The CPU side (prefix ``cpu_``) has a modified PoC.Mem interface, so that
17	-- this unit can be easily integrated into processor pipelines. For example,
18	-- let's have a pipeline where a load/store instruction is executed in 3
19	-- stages (after fetching, decoding, ...):
20	--
21	-- 1. Execute (EX) for address calculation,
22	-- 2. Load/Store 1 (LS1) for the cache access,
23	-- 3. Load/Store 2 (LS2) where the cache returns the read data.
24	--
25	-- The read data is always returned one cycle after the cache access completes,
26	-- so there is conceptually a pipeline register within this unit. The stage LS2
27	-- can be merged with a write-back stage if the clock period allows so.
28	--
29	-- The stage LS1 and thus EX and LS2 must stall, until the cache access is
30	-- completed, i.e., the EX/LS1 pipeline register must hold the cache request
31	-- until it is acknowledged by the cache. This is signaled by ``cpu_got`` as
32	-- described in Section Operation below. The pipeline moves forward (is
33	-- enabled) when::
34	--
35	--   pipeline_enable <= (not cpu_req) or cpu_got;
36	--
37	-- If the pipeline can stall due to other reasons, care must be taken to not
38	-- unintentionally executing the cache access twice or missing the read data.
39	--
40	-- Of course, the EX/LS1 pipeline register can be omitted and the CPU side
41	-- directly fed by the address caculator. But be aware of the high setup time
42	-- of this unit and high propate time for ``cpu_got``.
43	--
44	-- This unit supports only one outstanding CPU request. More outstanding
45	-- requests are provided by :ref:`IP:cache_mem`.
46	--
47	--
48	-- Configuration
49	-- *************
50	--
51	-- +--------------------+-----------------------------------------------------+
52	-- | Parameter          | Description                                         |
53	-- +====================+=====================================================+
54	-- | REPLACEMENT_POLICY | Replacement policy of embedded cache. For supported |
55	-- |                    | values see PoC.cache_replacement_policy.            |
56	-- +--------------------+-----------------------------------------------------+
57	-- | CACHE_LINES        | Number of cache lines.                              |
58	-- +--------------------+-----------------------------------------------------+
59	-- | ASSOCIATIVITY      | Associativity of embedded cache.                    |
60	-- +--------------------+-----------------------------------------------------+
61	-- | CPU_ADDR_BITS      | Number of address bits on the CPU side. Each address|
62	-- |                    | identifies one memory word as seen from the CPU.    |
63	-- |                    | Calculated from other parameters as described below.|
64	-- +--------------------+-----------------------------------------------------+
65	-- | CPU_DATA_BITS      | Width of the data bus (in bits) on the CPU side.    |
66	-- |                    | CPU_DATA_BITS must be divisible by 8.               |
67	-- +--------------------+-----------------------------------------------------+
68	-- | MEM_ADDR_BITS      | Number of address bits on the memory side. Each     |
69	-- |                    | address identifies one word in the memory.          |
70	-- +--------------------+-----------------------------------------------------+
71	-- | MEM_DATA_BITS      | Width of a memory word and of a cache line in bits. |
72	-- |                    | MEM_DATA_BITS must be divisible by CPU_DATA_BITS.   |
73	-- +--------------------+-----------------------------------------------------+
74	--
75	-- If the CPU data-bus width is smaller than the memory data-bus width, then
76	-- the CPU needs additional address bits to identify one CPU data word inside a
77	-- memory word. Thus, the CPU address-bus width is calculated from::
78	--
79	--   CPU_ADDR_BITS=log2ceil(MEM_DATA_BITS/CPU_DATA_BITS)+MEM_ADDR_BITS
80	--
81	-- The write policy is: write-through, no-write-allocate.
82	--
83	--
84	-- Operation
85	-- *********
86	--
87	-- Alignment of Cache / Memory Accesses
88	-- ++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++
89	--
90	-- Memory accesses are always aligned to a word boundary. Each memory word
91	-- (and each cache line) consists of MEM_DATA_BITS bits.
92	-- For example if MEM_DATA_BITS=128:
93	--
94	-- * memory address 0 selects the bits   0..127 in memory,
95	-- * memory address 1 selects the bits 128..256 in memory, and so on.
96	--
97	-- Cache accesses are always aligned to a CPU word boundary. Each CPU word
98	-- consists of CPU_DATA_BITS bits. For example if CPU_DATA_BITS=32:
99	--
100	-- * CPU address 0 selects the bits   0.. 31 in memory word 0,
101	-- * CPU address 1 selects the bits  32.. 63 in memory word 0,
102	-- * CPU address 2 selects the bits  64.. 95 in memory word 0,
103	-- * CPU address 3 selects the bits  96..127 in memory word 0,
104	-- * CPU address 4 selects the bits   0.. 31 in memory word 1,
105	-- * CPU address 5 selects the bits  32.. 63 in memory word 1, and so on.
106	--
107	--
108	-- Shared and Memory Side Interface
109	-- ++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++
110	--
111	-- A synchronous reset must be applied even on a FPGA.
112	--
113	-- The memory side interface is documented in detail :ref:`here <INT:PoC.Mem>`.
114	--
115	--
116	-- CPU Side Interface
117	-- ++++++++++++++++++
118	--
119	-- The CPU (pipeline stage LS1, see above) issues a request by setting
120	-- ``cpu_req``, ``cpu_write``, ``cpu_addr``, ``cpu_wdata`` and ``cpu_wmask`` as
121	-- in the :ref:`INT:PoC.Mem` interface. The cache acknowledges the request by
122	-- setting ``cpu_got`` to '1'. If the request is not acknowledged (``cpu_got =
123	-- '0'``) in the current clock cycle, then the request must be repeated in the
124	-- following clock cycle(s) until it is acknowledged, i.e., the pipeline must
125	-- stall.
126	--
127	-- A cache access is completed when it is acknowledged. A new request can be
128	-- issued in the following clock cycle.
129	--
130	-- Of course, ``cpu_got`` may be asserted in the same clock cycle where the
131	-- request was issued if a read hit occurs. This allows a throughput of one
132	-- (read) request per clock cycle, but the drawback is, that ``cpu_got`` has a
133	-- high propagation delay. Thus, this output should only control a simple
134	-- pipeline enable logic.
135	--
136	-- When ``cpu_got`` is asserted for a read access, then the read data will be
137	-- available in the following clock cycle.
138	--
139	-- Due to the write-through policy, a write will always take several clock
140	-- cycles and acknowledged when the data has been issued to the memory.
141	--
142	-- .. WARNING::
143	--
144	--    If the design is synthesized with Xilinx ISE / XST, then the synthesis
145	--    option "Keep Hierarchy" must be set to SOFT or TRUE.
146	--
147	-- SeeAlso:
148	--   :ref:`IP:cache_mem`
149	--
150	-- License:
151	-- =============================================================================
152	-- Copyright 2016-2016 Technische Universitaet Dresden - Germany
153	--										 Chair of VLSI-Design, Diagnostics and Architecture
154	--
155	-- Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
156	-- you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
157	-- You may obtain a copy of the License at
158	--
159	--		http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
160	--
161	-- Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
162	-- distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
163	-- WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
164	-- See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
165	-- limitations under the License.
166	-- =============================================================================
167	
168	library ieee;
169	use ieee.std_logic_1164.all;
170	use ieee.numeric_std.all;
171	
172	library poc;
173	use poc.utils.all;
174	
175	entity cache_cpu is
176		generic (
177			REPLACEMENT_POLICY : string		:= "LRU";
178			CACHE_LINES        : positive;
179			ASSOCIATIVITY      : positive;
180			CPU_DATA_BITS      : positive;
181			MEM_ADDR_BITS      : positive;
182			MEM_DATA_BITS      : positive
183		);
184		port (
185	    clk : in std_logic; -- clock
186	    rst : in std_logic; -- reset
187	
188	    -- "CPU" side
189	    cpu_req   : in  std_logic;
190	    cpu_write : in  std_logic;
191	    cpu_addr  : in  unsigned(log2ceil(MEM_DATA_BITS/CPU_DATA_BITS)+MEM_ADDR_BITS-1 downto 0);
192	    cpu_wdata : in  std_logic_vector(CPU_DATA_BITS-1 downto 0);
193	    cpu_wmask : in  std_logic_vector(CPU_DATA_BITS/8-1 downto 0);
194	    cpu_got   : out std_logic;
195	    cpu_rdata : out std_logic_vector(CPU_DATA_BITS-1 downto 0);
196	
197			-- Memory side
198			mem_req		: out std_logic;
199			mem_write : out std_logic;
200			mem_addr	: out unsigned(MEM_ADDR_BITS-1 downto 0);
201			mem_wdata : out std_logic_vector(MEM_DATA_BITS-1 downto 0);
202			mem_wmask : out std_logic_vector(MEM_DATA_BITS/8-1 downto 0);
203			mem_rdy		: in	std_logic;
204			mem_rstb	: in	std_logic;
205			mem_rdata : in	std_logic_vector(MEM_DATA_BITS-1 downto 0)
206	    );
207	end entity;
208	
209	architecture rtl of cache_cpu is
210		-- Ratio 1:n between CPU data bus and cache-line size (memory data bus)
211		constant RATIO : positive := MEM_DATA_BITS/CPU_DATA_BITS;
212	
213		-- Number of address bits identifying the CPU data word within a cache line (memory word)
214		constant LOWER_ADDR_BITS : natural := log2ceil(RATIO);
215	
216		-- Widened CPU data path
217		signal cpu_wdata_wide : std_logic_vector(MEM_DATA_BITS-1 downto 0);
218		signal cpu_wmask_wide : std_logic_vector(MEM_DATA_BITS/8-1 downto 0);
219	
220		-- Interface to Cache instance.
221		signal cache_Request		: std_logic;
222		signal cache_ReadWrite	: std_logic;
223		signal cache_Writemask	: std_logic_vector(MEM_DATA_BITS/8-1 downto 0);
224		signal cache_Invalidate : std_logic;
225		signal cache_Replace		: std_logic;
226		signal cache_Address		: std_logic_vector(MEM_ADDR_BITS-1 downto 0);
227		signal cache_LineIn			: std_logic_vector(MEM_DATA_BITS-1 downto 0);
228		signal cache_LineOut		: std_logic_vector(MEM_DATA_BITS-1 downto 0);
229		signal cache_Hit				: std_logic;
230		signal cache_Miss				: std_logic;
231	
232	  -- FSM and other state registers
233	  type T_FSM is (READY, ACCESS_MEM, READING_MEM, UNKNOWN);
234	  signal fsm_cs : T_FSM -- current state
235			-- synthesis translate_off
236			:= UNKNOWN
237			-- synthesis translate_on
238			;
239	  signal fsm_ns : T_FSM;-- next state
240	
241	begin  -- architecture rtl
242	
243	  cache_inst: entity work.cache_par2
244	    generic map (
245				REPLACEMENT_POLICY => REPLACEMENT_POLICY,
246				CACHE_LINES        => CACHE_LINES,
247				ASSOCIATIVITY      => ASSOCIATIVITY,
248				ADDR_BITS          => MEM_ADDR_BITS,
249				DATA_BITS          => MEM_DATA_BITS,
250				HIT_MISS_REG       => false)
251	    port map (
252				Clock        => clk,
253				Reset        => rst,
254				Request      => cache_Request,
255				ReadWrite    => cache_ReadWrite,
256				WriteMask    => cache_WriteMask,
257				Invalidate   => cache_Invalidate,
258				Replace      => cache_Replace,
259				Address      => cache_Address,
260				CacheLineIn  => cache_LineIn,
261				CacheLineOut => cache_LineOut,
262				CacheHit     => cache_Hit,
263				CacheMiss    => cache_Miss,
264				OldAddress   => open);
265	
266	  -- Address and Data path
267	  -- ===========================================================================
268		gEqual: if RATIO = 1 generate -- Cache line size equals CPU data bus size
269			cpu_wdata_wide <= cpu_wdata;
270			cpu_wmask_wide <= cpu_wmask;
271			cpu_rdata      <= cache_LineOut;
272		end generate gEqual;
273	
274		gWider: if RATIO > 1 generate -- Cache line size is greater than CPU data bus size
275			signal lower_addr   : unsigned(LOWER_ADDR_BITS-1 downto 0);
276			signal lower_addr_r : unsigned(LOWER_ADDR_BITS-1 downto 0);
277			type T_ARRAY is array(0 to RATIO-1) of std_logic_vector(CPU_DATA_BITS-1 downto 0);
278			signal cache_LineOut_array : T_ARRAY;
279		begin
280			-- CPU Request Data Path
281			lower_addr <= cpu_addr(LOWER_ADDR_BITS-1 downto 0);
282	
283			l0: for i in 0 to RATIO-1 generate
284				cpu_wdata_wide((i+1)*CPU_DATA_BITS-1 downto i*CPU_DATA_BITS) <= cpu_wdata;
285	
286				cpu_wmask_wide((i+1)*CPU_DATA_BITS/8-1 downto i*CPU_DATA_BITS/8) <=
287					-- synthesis translate_off
288					(others => 'X') when is_x(lower_addr) else
289					-- synthesis translate_on
290					cpu_wmask when to_integer(lower_addr) = i else
291					(others => '1');
292			end generate l0;
293	
294			-- CPU Reply Data Path
295			lower_addr_r <= lower_addr when rising_edge(clk); -- pipeline register
296	
297			l1: for i in 0 to RATIO-1 generate
298				cache_LineOut_array(i) <= cache_LineOut((i+1)*CPU_DATA_BITS-1 downto i*CPU_DATA_BITS);
299			end generate l1;
300	
301			cpu_rdata <=
302				-- synthesis translate_off
303				(others => 'X') when is_x(lower_addr_r) else
304				-- synthesis translate_on
305				cache_LineOut_array(to_integer(lower_addr_r));
306		end generate gWider;
307	
308		-- Cache Request Data Path
309		cache_Address   <= std_logic_vector(cpu_addr(cpu_addr'left downto LOWER_ADDR_BITS));
310		cache_LineIn    <= mem_rdata       when fsm_cs = READING_MEM else cpu_wdata_wide;
311		cache_WriteMask <= (others => '0') when fsm_cs = READING_MEM else cpu_wmask_wide;
312	
313		-- These outputs can be fed from buffer registers, but this is not
314		-- neccessary because the cpu_* signals will typically be connected to a
315		-- pipeline register. And even if this pipeline register is omitted, then the
316		-- cache tag comparison will dominate the critical path.
317		mem_write <= cpu_write;
318		mem_addr  <= cpu_addr(cpu_addr'left downto LOWER_ADDR_BITS);
319		mem_wdata <= cpu_wdata_wide;
320		mem_wmask <= cpu_wmask_wide;
321	
322		-- FSM
323		-- ===========================================================================
324		process(fsm_cs, cpu_req, cpu_write, cache_Hit, cache_Miss, mem_rdy, mem_rstb)
325		begin
326			-- Update state registers
327			fsm_ns <= fsm_cs;
328	
329			-- Control signals for cache access
330			cache_Request		 <= '0';
331			cache_ReadWrite	 <= '-';
332			cache_Invalidate <= '-';
333			cache_Replace		 <= '0';
334	
335			-- Control / status signals for CPU and MEM side
336			cpu_got <= '0';
337			mem_req <= '0';
338	
339			case fsm_cs is
340				when READY =>
341					-- Ready for a new cache access.
342					-- -----------------------------
343					cache_Request		 <= to_x01(cpu_req);
344					cache_ReadWrite	 <= to_x01(cpu_write); -- doesn't care if no request
345					cache_Invalidate <= '0';
346	
347					case ((cache_Hit and cpu_write) or cache_Miss) is
348						when '1' =>	-- write successfull but write-through, or cache miss
349							fsm_ns  <= ACCESS_MEM;
350						when '0' => -- read successfull, or no request
351							cpu_got <= to_x01(cpu_req);
352						when others => -- invalid input
353							fsm_ns  <= UNKNOWN;
354							cpu_got <= 'X';
355					end case;
356	
357	
358				when ACCESS_MEM =>
359					-- Access memory.
360					-- --------------
361					mem_req <= '1';
362					case to_x01(mem_rdy) is
363						when '1' => -- access granted
364							case to_x01(cpu_write) is
365								when '1'    => fsm_ns <= READY;   cpu_got <= '1'; -- write
366								when '0'    => fsm_ns <= READING_MEM; -- read
367								when others => fsm_ns <= UNKNOWN; cpu_got <= 'X'; -- invalid input
368							end case;
369	
370						when '0' =>	null; -- still waiting
371						when others => fsm_ns <= UNKNOWN; -- invalid input
372					end case;
373	
374	
375	      when READING_MEM =>
376	        -- Wait for incoming read data and write it to cache.
377					-- --------------------------------------------------
378					cache_ReadWrite <= '1';
379	
380					case to_x01(mem_rstb) is
381						when '1' => -- read data available
382							fsm_ns        <= READY;
383							cpu_got       <= '1'; -- cache access is complete now
384							cache_Replace <= '1'; -- replace cache line
385							-- The new data will be available on cache_LineOut in the following
386							-- clock cycle.
387	
388						when '0' => null;-- still waiting
389						when others => -- invalid input
390							fsm_ns        <= UNKNOWN;
391							cpu_got       <= 'X';
392							cache_Replace <= 'X';
393					end case;
394	
395	
396				when UNKNOWN =>
397					-- Catches invalid state transitions.
398					-- ----------------------------------
399					fsm_ns           <= UNKNOWN;
400					cpu_got          <= 'X';
401					cache_Request    <= 'X';
402					cache_ReadWrite	 <= 'X';
403					cache_Invalidate <= 'X';
404					cache_Replace    <= 'X';
405			end case;
406		end process;
407	
408		process(clk)
409		begin
410			if rising_edge(clk) then
411				case to_x01(rst) is
412					when '1' =>
413						fsm_cs <= READY;
414					when '0' =>
415						fsm_cs <= fsm_ns;
416					when others =>
417						fsm_cs <= UNKNOWN;
418				end case;
419			end if;
420		end process;
421	
422	end architecture rtl;
423

-- EMACS settings: -*-  tab-width: 2; indent-tabs-mode: t -*-
-- vim: tabstop=2:shiftwidth=2:noexpandtab
-- kate: tab-width 2; replace-tabs off; indent-width 2;
-- =============================================================================
-- Authors:         Martin Zabel
--
-- Entity:          Cache with cache controller to be used within a CPU
--
-- Description:
-- -------------------------------------
-- This unit provides a cache (:ref:`IP:cache_par2`) together
-- with a cache controller which reads / writes cache lines from / to memory.
-- The memory is accessed using a :ref:`INT:PoC.Mem` interfaces, the related
-- ports and parameters are prefixed with ``mem_``.
--
-- The CPU side (prefix ``cpu_``) has a modified PoC.Mem interface, so that
-- this unit can be easily integrated into processor pipelines. For example,
-- let's have a pipeline where a load/store instruction is executed in 3
-- stages (after fetching, decoding, ...):
--
-- 1. Execute (EX) for address calculation,
-- 2. Load/Store 1 (LS1) for the cache access,
-- 3. Load/Store 2 (LS2) where the cache returns the read data.
--
-- The read data is always returned one cycle after the cache access completes,
-- so there is conceptually a pipeline register within this unit. The stage LS2
-- can be merged with a write-back stage if the clock period allows so.
--
-- The stage LS1 and thus EX and LS2 must stall, until the cache access is
-- completed, i.e., the EX/LS1 pipeline register must hold the cache request
-- until it is acknowledged by the cache. This is signaled by ``cpu_got`` as
-- described in Section Operation below. The pipeline moves forward (is
-- enabled) when::
--
--   pipeline_enable <= (not cpu_req) or cpu_got;
--
-- If the pipeline can stall due to other reasons, care must be taken to not
-- unintentionally executing the cache access twice or missing the read data.
--
-- Of course, the EX/LS1 pipeline register can be omitted and the CPU side
-- directly fed by the address caculator. But be aware of the high setup time
-- of this unit and high propate time for ``cpu_got``.
--
-- This unit supports only one outstanding CPU request. More outstanding
-- requests are provided by :ref:`IP:cache_mem`.
--
--
-- Configuration
-- *************
--
-- +--------------------+-----------------------------------------------------+
-- | Parameter          | Description                                         |
-- +====================+=====================================================+
-- | REPLACEMENT_POLICY | Replacement policy of embedded cache. For supported |
-- |                    | values see PoC.cache_replacement_policy.            |
-- +--------------------+-----------------------------------------------------+
-- | CACHE_LINES        | Number of cache lines.                              |
-- +--------------------+-----------------------------------------------------+
-- | ASSOCIATIVITY      | Associativity of embedded cache.                    |
-- +--------------------+-----------------------------------------------------+
-- | CPU_ADDR_BITS      | Number of address bits on the CPU side. Each address|
-- |                    | identifies one memory word as seen from the CPU.    |
-- |                    | Calculated from other parameters as described below.|
-- +--------------------+-----------------------------------------------------+
-- | CPU_DATA_BITS      | Width of the data bus (in bits) on the CPU side.    |
-- |                    | CPU_DATA_BITS must be divisible by 8.               |
-- +--------------------+-----------------------------------------------------+
-- | MEM_ADDR_BITS      | Number of address bits on the memory side. Each     |
-- |                    | address identifies one word in the memory.          |
-- +--------------------+-----------------------------------------------------+
-- | MEM_DATA_BITS      | Width of a memory word and of a cache line in bits. |
-- |                    | MEM_DATA_BITS must be divisible by CPU_DATA_BITS.   |
-- +--------------------+-----------------------------------------------------+
--
-- If the CPU data-bus width is smaller than the memory data-bus width, then
-- the CPU needs additional address bits to identify one CPU data word inside a
-- memory word. Thus, the CPU address-bus width is calculated from::
--
--   CPU_ADDR_BITS=log2ceil(MEM_DATA_BITS/CPU_DATA_BITS)+MEM_ADDR_BITS
--
-- The write policy is: write-through, no-write-allocate.
--
--
-- Operation
-- *********
--
-- Alignment of Cache / Memory Accesses
-- ++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++
--
-- Memory accesses are always aligned to a word boundary. Each memory word
-- (and each cache line) consists of MEM_DATA_BITS bits.
-- For example if MEM_DATA_BITS=128:
--
-- * memory address 0 selects the bits   0..127 in memory,
-- * memory address 1 selects the bits 128..256 in memory, and so on.
--
-- Cache accesses are always aligned to a CPU word boundary. Each CPU word
-- consists of CPU_DATA_BITS bits. For example if CPU_DATA_BITS=32:
--
-- * CPU address 0 selects the bits   0.. 31 in memory word 0,
-- * CPU address 1 selects the bits  32.. 63 in memory word 0,
-- * CPU address 2 selects the bits  64.. 95 in memory word 0,
-- * CPU address 3 selects the bits  96..127 in memory word 0,
-- * CPU address 4 selects the bits   0.. 31 in memory word 1,
-- * CPU address 5 selects the bits  32.. 63 in memory word 1, and so on.
--
--
-- Shared and Memory Side Interface
-- ++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++
--
-- A synchronous reset must be applied even on a FPGA.
--
-- The memory side interface is documented in detail :ref:`here <INT:PoC.Mem>`.
--
--
-- CPU Side Interface
-- ++++++++++++++++++
--
-- The CPU (pipeline stage LS1, see above) issues a request by setting
-- ``cpu_req``, ``cpu_write``, ``cpu_addr``, ``cpu_wdata`` and ``cpu_wmask`` as
-- in the :ref:`INT:PoC.Mem` interface. The cache acknowledges the request by
-- setting ``cpu_got`` to '1'. If the request is not acknowledged (``cpu_got =
-- '0'``) in the current clock cycle, then the request must be repeated in the
-- following clock cycle(s) until it is acknowledged, i.e., the pipeline must
-- stall.
--
-- A cache access is completed when it is acknowledged. A new request can be
-- issued in the following clock cycle.
--
-- Of course, ``cpu_got`` may be asserted in the same clock cycle where the
-- request was issued if a read hit occurs. This allows a throughput of one
-- (read) request per clock cycle, but the drawback is, that ``cpu_got`` has a
-- high propagation delay. Thus, this output should only control a simple
-- pipeline enable logic.
--
-- When ``cpu_got`` is asserted for a read access, then the read data will be
-- available in the following clock cycle.
--
-- Due to the write-through policy, a write will always take several clock
-- cycles and acknowledged when the data has been issued to the memory.
--
-- .. WARNING::
--
--    If the design is synthesized with Xilinx ISE / XST, then the synthesis
--    option "Keep Hierarchy" must be set to SOFT or TRUE.
--
-- SeeAlso:
--   :ref:`IP:cache_mem`
--
-- License:
-- =============================================================================
-- Copyright 2016-2016 Technische Universitaet Dresden - Germany
--										 Chair of VLSI-Design, Diagnostics and Architecture
--
-- Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
-- you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
-- You may obtain a copy of the License at
--
--		http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
--
-- Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
-- distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
-- WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
-- See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
-- limitations under the License.
-- =============================================================================

library ieee;
use ieee.std_logic_1164.all;
use ieee.numeric_std.all;

library poc;
use poc.utils.all;

entity cache_cpu is
	generic (
		REPLACEMENT_POLICY : string		:= "LRU";
		CACHE_LINES        : positive;
		ASSOCIATIVITY      : positive;
		CPU_DATA_BITS      : positive;
		MEM_ADDR_BITS      : positive;
		MEM_DATA_BITS      : positive
	);
	port (
    clk : in std_logic; -- clock
    rst : in std_logic; -- reset

    -- "CPU" side
    cpu_req   : in  std_logic;
    cpu_write : in  std_logic;
    cpu_addr  : in  unsigned(log2ceil(MEM_DATA_BITS/CPU_DATA_BITS)+MEM_ADDR_BITS-1 downto 0);
    cpu_wdata : in  std_logic_vector(CPU_DATA_BITS-1 downto 0);
    cpu_wmask : in  std_logic_vector(CPU_DATA_BITS/8-1 downto 0);
    cpu_got   : out std_logic;
    cpu_rdata : out std_logic_vector(CPU_DATA_BITS-1 downto 0);

		-- Memory side
		mem_req		: out std_logic;
		mem_write : out std_logic;
		mem_addr	: out unsigned(MEM_ADDR_BITS-1 downto 0);
		mem_wdata : out std_logic_vector(MEM_DATA_BITS-1 downto 0);
		mem_wmask : out std_logic_vector(MEM_DATA_BITS/8-1 downto 0);
		mem_rdy		: in	std_logic;
		mem_rstb	: in	std_logic;
		mem_rdata : in	std_logic_vector(MEM_DATA_BITS-1 downto 0)
    );
end entity;

architecture rtl of cache_cpu is
	-- Ratio 1:n between CPU data bus and cache-line size (memory data bus)
	constant RATIO : positive := MEM_DATA_BITS/CPU_DATA_BITS;

	-- Number of address bits identifying the CPU data word within a cache line (memory word)
	constant LOWER_ADDR_BITS : natural := log2ceil(RATIO);

	-- Widened CPU data path
	signal cpu_wdata_wide : std_logic_vector(MEM_DATA_BITS-1 downto 0);
	signal cpu_wmask_wide : std_logic_vector(MEM_DATA_BITS/8-1 downto 0);

	-- Interface to Cache instance.
	signal cache_Request		: std_logic;
	signal cache_ReadWrite	: std_logic;
	signal cache_Writemask	: std_logic_vector(MEM_DATA_BITS/8-1 downto 0);
	signal cache_Invalidate : std_logic;
	signal cache_Replace		: std_logic;
	signal cache_Address		: std_logic_vector(MEM_ADDR_BITS-1 downto 0);
	signal cache_LineIn			: std_logic_vector(MEM_DATA_BITS-1 downto 0);
	signal cache_LineOut		: std_logic_vector(MEM_DATA_BITS-1 downto 0);
	signal cache_Hit				: std_logic;
	signal cache_Miss				: std_logic;

  -- FSM and other state registers
  type T_FSM is (READY, ACCESS_MEM, READING_MEM, UNKNOWN);
  signal fsm_cs : T_FSM -- current state
		-- synthesis translate_off
		:= UNKNOWN
		-- synthesis translate_on
		;
  signal fsm_ns : T_FSM;-- next state

begin  -- architecture rtl

  cache_inst: entity work.cache_par2
    generic map (
			REPLACEMENT_POLICY => REPLACEMENT_POLICY,
			CACHE_LINES        => CACHE_LINES,
			ASSOCIATIVITY      => ASSOCIATIVITY,
			ADDR_BITS          => MEM_ADDR_BITS,
			DATA_BITS          => MEM_DATA_BITS,
			HIT_MISS_REG       => false)
    port map (
			Clock        => clk,
			Reset        => rst,
			Request      => cache_Request,
			ReadWrite    => cache_ReadWrite,
			WriteMask    => cache_WriteMask,
			Invalidate   => cache_Invalidate,
			Replace      => cache_Replace,
			Address      => cache_Address,
			CacheLineIn  => cache_LineIn,
			CacheLineOut => cache_LineOut,
			CacheHit     => cache_Hit,
			CacheMiss    => cache_Miss,
			OldAddress   => open);

  -- Address and Data path
  -- ===========================================================================
	gEqual: if RATIO = 1 generate -- Cache line size equals CPU data bus size
		cpu_wdata_wide <= cpu_wdata;
		cpu_wmask_wide <= cpu_wmask;
		cpu_rdata      <= cache_LineOut;
	end generate gEqual;

	gWider: if RATIO > 1 generate -- Cache line size is greater than CPU data bus size
		signal lower_addr   : unsigned(LOWER_ADDR_BITS-1 downto 0);
		signal lower_addr_r : unsigned(LOWER_ADDR_BITS-1 downto 0);
		type T_ARRAY is array(0 to RATIO-1) of std_logic_vector(CPU_DATA_BITS-1 downto 0);
		signal cache_LineOut_array : T_ARRAY;
	begin
		-- CPU Request Data Path
		lower_addr <= cpu_addr(LOWER_ADDR_BITS-1 downto 0);

		l0: for i in 0 to RATIO-1 generate
			cpu_wdata_wide((i+1)*CPU_DATA_BITS-1 downto i*CPU_DATA_BITS) <= cpu_wdata;

			cpu_wmask_wide((i+1)*CPU_DATA_BITS/8-1 downto i*CPU_DATA_BITS/8) <=
				-- synthesis translate_off
				(others => 'X') when is_x(lower_addr) else
				-- synthesis translate_on
				cpu_wmask when to_integer(lower_addr) = i else
				(others => '1');
		end generate l0;

		-- CPU Reply Data Path
		lower_addr_r <= lower_addr when rising_edge(clk); -- pipeline register

		l1: for i in 0 to RATIO-1 generate
			cache_LineOut_array(i) <= cache_LineOut((i+1)*CPU_DATA_BITS-1 downto i*CPU_DATA_BITS);
		end generate l1;

		cpu_rdata <=
			-- synthesis translate_off
			(others => 'X') when is_x(lower_addr_r) else
			-- synthesis translate_on
			cache_LineOut_array(to_integer(lower_addr_r));
	end generate gWider;

	-- Cache Request Data Path
	cache_Address   <= std_logic_vector(cpu_addr(cpu_addr'left downto LOWER_ADDR_BITS));
	cache_LineIn    <= mem_rdata       when fsm_cs = READING_MEM else cpu_wdata_wide;
	cache_WriteMask <= (others => '0') when fsm_cs = READING_MEM else cpu_wmask_wide;

	-- These outputs can be fed from buffer registers, but this is not
	-- neccessary because the cpu_* signals will typically be connected to a
	-- pipeline register. And even if this pipeline register is omitted, then the
	-- cache tag comparison will dominate the critical path.
	mem_write <= cpu_write;
	mem_addr  <= cpu_addr(cpu_addr'left downto LOWER_ADDR_BITS);
	mem_wdata <= cpu_wdata_wide;
	mem_wmask <= cpu_wmask_wide;

	-- FSM
	-- ===========================================================================
	process(fsm_cs, cpu_req, cpu_write, cache_Hit, cache_Miss, mem_rdy, mem_rstb)
	begin
		-- Update state registers
		fsm_ns <= fsm_cs;

		-- Control signals for cache access
		cache_Request		 <= '0';
		cache_ReadWrite	 <= '-';
		cache_Invalidate <= '-';
		cache_Replace		 <= '0';

		-- Control / status signals for CPU and MEM side
		cpu_got <= '0';
		mem_req <= '0';

		case fsm_cs is
			when READY =>
				-- Ready for a new cache access.
				-- -----------------------------
				cache_Request		 <= to_x01(cpu_req);
				cache_ReadWrite	 <= to_x01(cpu_write); -- doesn't care if no request
				cache_Invalidate <= '0';

				case ((cache_Hit and cpu_write) or cache_Miss) is
					when '1' =>	-- write successfull but write-through, or cache miss
						fsm_ns  <= ACCESS_MEM;
					when '0' => -- read successfull, or no request
						cpu_got <= to_x01(cpu_req);
					when others => -- invalid input
						fsm_ns  <= UNKNOWN;
						cpu_got <= 'X';
				end case;


			when ACCESS_MEM =>
				-- Access memory.
				-- --------------
				mem_req <= '1';
				case to_x01(mem_rdy) is
					when '1' => -- access granted
						case to_x01(cpu_write) is
							when '1'    => fsm_ns <= READY;   cpu_got <= '1'; -- write
							when '0'    => fsm_ns <= READING_MEM; -- read
							when others => fsm_ns <= UNKNOWN; cpu_got <= 'X'; -- invalid input
						end case;

					when '0' =>	null; -- still waiting
					when others => fsm_ns <= UNKNOWN; -- invalid input
				end case;


      when READING_MEM =>
        -- Wait for incoming read data and write it to cache.
				-- --------------------------------------------------
				cache_ReadWrite <= '1';

				case to_x01(mem_rstb) is
					when '1' => -- read data available
						fsm_ns        <= READY;
						cpu_got       <= '1'; -- cache access is complete now
						cache_Replace <= '1'; -- replace cache line
						-- The new data will be available on cache_LineOut in the following
						-- clock cycle.

					when '0' => null;-- still waiting
					when others => -- invalid input
						fsm_ns        <= UNKNOWN;
						cpu_got       <= 'X';
						cache_Replace <= 'X';
				end case;


			when UNKNOWN =>
				-- Catches invalid state transitions.
				-- ----------------------------------
				fsm_ns           <= UNKNOWN;
				cpu_got          <= 'X';
				cache_Request    <= 'X';
				cache_ReadWrite	 <= 'X';
				cache_Invalidate <= 'X';
				cache_Replace    <= 'X';
		end case;
	end process;

	process(clk)
	begin
		if rising_edge(clk) then
			case to_x01(rst) is
				when '1' =>
					fsm_cs <= READY;
				when '0' =>
					fsm_cs <= fsm_ns;
				when others =>
					fsm_cs <= UNKNOWN;
			end case;
		end if;
	end process;

end architecture rtl;

content.content:
1	-- EMACS settings: -*-  tab-width: 2; indent-tabs-mode: t -*-
2	-- vim: tabstop=2:shiftwidth=2:noexpandtab
3	-- kate: tab-width 2; replace-tabs off; indent-width 2;
4	-- =============================================================================
5	-- Authors:         Martin Zabel
6	--
7	-- Entity:          Cache with :ref:`INT:PoC.Mem` interface on the "CPU" side
8	--
9	-- Description:
10	-- -------------------------------------
11	-- This unit provides a cache (:ref:`IP:cache_par2`) together
12	-- with a cache controller which reads / writes cache lines from / to memory.
13	-- It has two :ref:`INT:PoC.Mem` interfaces:
14	--
15	-- * one for the "CPU" side  (ports with prefix ``cpu_``), and
16	-- * one for the memory side (ports with prefix ``mem_``).
17	--
18	-- Thus, this unit can be placed into an already available memory path between
19	-- the CPU and the memory (controller). If you want to plugin a cache into a
20	-- CPU pipeline, see :ref:`IP:cache_cpu`.
21	--
22	--
23	-- Configuration
24	-- *************
25	--
26	-- +--------------------+-----------------------------------------------------+
27	-- | Parameter          | Description                                         |
28	-- +====================+=====================================================+
29	-- | REPLACEMENT_POLICY | Replacement policy of embedded cache. For supported |
30	-- |                    | values see PoC.cache_replacement_policy.            |
31	-- +--------------------+-----------------------------------------------------+
32	-- | CACHE_LINES        | Number of cache lines.                              |
33	-- +--------------------+-----------------------------------------------------+
34	-- | ASSOCIATIVITY      | Associativity of embedded cache.                    |
35	-- +--------------------+-----------------------------------------------------+
36	-- | CPU_ADDR_BITS      | Number of address bits on the CPU side. Each address|
37	-- |                    | identifies one memory word as seen from the CPU.    |
38	-- |                    | Calculated from other parameters as described below.|
39	-- +--------------------+-----------------------------------------------------+
40	-- | CPU_DATA_BITS      | Width of the data bus (in bits) on the CPU side.    |
41	-- |                    | CPU_DATA_BITS must be divisible by 8.               |
42	-- +--------------------+-----------------------------------------------------+
43	-- | MEM_ADDR_BITS      | Number of address bits on the memory side. Each     |
44	-- |                    | address identifies one word in the memory.          |
45	-- +--------------------+-----------------------------------------------------+
46	-- | MEM_DATA_BITS      | Width of a memory word and of a cache line in bits. |
47	-- |                    | MEM_DATA_BITS must be divisible by CPU_DATA_BITS.   |
48	-- +--------------------+-----------------------------------------------------+
49	-- | OUTSTANDING_REQ    | Number of oustanding requests, see notes below.     |
50	-- +--------------------+-----------------------------------------------------+
51	--
52	-- If the CPU data-bus width is smaller than the memory data-bus width, then
53	-- the CPU needs additional address bits to identify one CPU data word inside a
54	-- memory word. Thus, the CPU address-bus width is calculated from::
55	--
56	--   CPU_ADDR_BITS=log2ceil(MEM_DATA_BITS/CPU_DATA_BITS)+MEM_ADDR_BITS
57	--
58	-- The write policy is: write-through, no-write-allocate.
59	--
60	-- The maximum throughput is one request per clock cycle, except for
61	-- ``OUSTANDING_REQ = 1``.
62	--
63	-- If ``OUTSTANDING_REQ`` is:
64	--
65	-- * 1: then 1 request is buffered by a single register. To give a short
66	--   critical path (clock-to-output delay) for ``cpu_rdy``, the throughput is
67	--   degraded to one request per 2 clock cycles at maximum.
68	--
69	-- * 2: then 2 requests are buffered by :ref:`IP:fifo_glue`. This setting has
70	--   the lowest area requirements without degrading the performance.
71	--
72	-- * >2: then the requests are buffered by :ref:`IP:fifo_cc_got`. The number of
73	--   outstanding requests is rounded up to the next suitable value. This setting
74	--   is useful in applications with out-of-order execution (of other
75	--   operations). The CPU requests to the cache are always processed in-order.
76	--
77	--
78	-- Operation
79	-- *********
80	--
81	-- Memory accesses are always aligned to a word boundary. Each memory word
82	-- (and each cache line) consists of MEM_DATA_BITS bits.
83	-- For example if MEM_DATA_BITS=128:
84	--
85	-- * memory address 0 selects the bits   0..127 in memory,
86	-- * memory address 1 selects the bits 128..256 in memory, and so on.
87	--
88	-- Cache accesses are always aligned to a CPU word boundary. Each CPU word
89	-- consists of CPU_DATA_BITS bits. For example if CPU_DATA_BITS=32:
90	--
91	-- * CPU address 0 selects the bits   0.. 31 in memory word 0,
92	-- * CPU address 1 selects the bits  32.. 63 in memory word 0,
93	-- * CPU address 2 selects the bits  64.. 95 in memory word 0,
94	-- * CPU address 3 selects the bits  96..127 in memory word 0,
95	-- * CPU address 4 selects the bits   0.. 31 in memory word 1,
96	-- * CPU address 5 selects the bits  32.. 63 in memory word 1, and so on.
97	--
98	-- A synchronous reset must be applied even on a FPGA.
99	--
100	-- The interface is documented in detail :ref:`here <INT:PoC.Mem>`.
101	--
102	-- .. WARNING::
103	--
104	--    If the design is synthesized with Xilinx ISE / XST, then the synthesis
105	--    option "Keep Hierarchy" must be set to SOFT or TRUE.
106	--
107	-- SeeAlso:
108	--   :ref:`IP:cache_cpu`
109	--
110	-- License:
111	-- =============================================================================
112	-- Copyright 2016-2016 Technische Universitaet Dresden - Germany
113	--										 Chair of VLSI-Design, Diagnostics and Architecture
114	--
115	-- Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
116	-- you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
117	-- You may obtain a copy of the License at
118	--
119	--		http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
120	--
121	-- Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
122	-- distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
123	-- WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
124	-- See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
125	-- limitations under the License.
126	-- =============================================================================
127	
128	library ieee;
129	use ieee.std_logic_1164.all;
130	use ieee.numeric_std.all;
131	
132	library poc;
133	use poc.utils.all;
134	
135	entity cache_mem is
136		generic (
137			REPLACEMENT_POLICY : string		:= "LRU";
138			CACHE_LINES        : positive;
139			ASSOCIATIVITY      : positive;
140			CPU_DATA_BITS      : positive;
141			MEM_ADDR_BITS      : positive;
142			MEM_DATA_BITS      : positive;
143			OUTSTANDING_REQ    : positive := 2
144		);
145		port (
146			clk : in std_logic; -- clock
147			rst : in std_logic; -- reset
148	
149			-- "CPU" side
150			cpu_req   : in  std_logic;
151			cpu_write : in  std_logic;
152			cpu_addr  : in  unsigned(log2ceil(MEM_DATA_BITS/CPU_DATA_BITS)+MEM_ADDR_BITS-1 downto 0);
153			cpu_wdata : in  std_logic_vector(CPU_DATA_BITS-1 downto 0);
154			cpu_wmask : in  std_logic_vector(CPU_DATA_BITS/8-1 downto 0) := (others => '0');
155			cpu_rdy   : out std_logic;
156			cpu_rstb  : out std_logic;
157			cpu_rdata : out std_logic_vector(CPU_DATA_BITS-1 downto 0);
158	
159			-- Memory side
160			mem_req   : out std_logic;
161			mem_write : out std_logic;
162			mem_addr  : out unsigned(MEM_ADDR_BITS-1 downto 0);
163			mem_wdata : out std_logic_vector(MEM_DATA_BITS-1 downto 0);
164			mem_wmask : out std_logic_vector(MEM_DATA_BITS/8-1 downto 0);
165			mem_rdy   : in  std_logic;
166			mem_rstb  : in  std_logic;
167			mem_rdata : in  std_logic_vector(MEM_DATA_BITS-1 downto 0)
168	    );
169	end entity;
170	
171	architecture rtl of cache_mem is
172		constant CPU_ADDR_BITS : positive := log2ceil(MEM_DATA_BITS/CPU_DATA_BITS)+MEM_ADDR_BITS;
173	
174		-- signals to internal cache_cpu
175		signal int_req   : std_logic;
176		signal int_write : std_logic;
177		signal int_addr  : unsigned(CPU_ADDR_BITS-1 downto 0);
178		signal int_wdata : std_logic_vector(CPU_DATA_BITS-1 downto 0);
179		signal int_wmask : std_logic_vector(CPU_DATA_BITS/8-1 downto 0);
180		signal int_got   : std_logic;
181		signal int_rdata : std_logic_vector(CPU_DATA_BITS-1 downto 0);
182	
183	begin
184	
185		cache_cpu_inst: entity work.cache_cpu
186			generic map (
187				REPLACEMENT_POLICY => REPLACEMENT_POLICY,
188				CACHE_LINES        => CACHE_LINES,
189				ASSOCIATIVITY      => ASSOCIATIVITY,
190				CPU_DATA_BITS      => CPU_DATA_BITS,
191				MEM_ADDR_BITS      => MEM_ADDR_BITS,
192				MEM_DATA_BITS      => MEM_DATA_BITS)
193			port map (
194				clk       => clk,
195				rst       => rst,
196				cpu_req   => int_req,
197				cpu_write => int_write,
198				cpu_addr  => int_addr,
199				cpu_wdata => int_wdata,
200				cpu_wmask => int_wmask,
201				cpu_got   => int_got,
202				cpu_rdata => int_rdata,
203				mem_req   => mem_req,
204				mem_write => mem_write,
205				mem_addr  => mem_addr,
206				mem_wdata => mem_wdata,
207				mem_wmask => mem_wmask,
208				mem_rdy   => mem_rdy,
209				mem_rstb  => mem_rstb,
210				mem_rdata => mem_rdata);
211	
212		-- read data is valid one clock cycle after int_got is asserted
213		cpu_rstb  <= (not rst) and (not int_write) and int_got when rising_edge(clk);
214		cpu_rdata <= int_rdata; -- already delayed by one clock cycle
215	
216	
217		------------------------------------------------------------------------------
218		g1: if OUTSTANDING_REQ = 1 generate
219	    signal cpu_req_r   : std_logic;
220	    signal cpu_write_r : std_logic;
221	    signal cpu_addr_r  : unsigned(CPU_ADDR_BITS-1 downto 0);
222	    signal cpu_wdata_r : std_logic_vector(CPU_DATA_BITS-1 downto 0);
223	    signal cpu_wmask_r : std_logic_vector(CPU_DATA_BITS/8-1 downto 0);
224	    signal cpu_rdy_r   : std_logic;
225	    signal cpu_rstb_r  : std_logic;
226	    signal cpu_rdata_r : std_logic_vector(CPU_DATA_BITS-1 downto 0);
227		begin
228			-- cpu_rdy should have a short clock-to-output delay, but int_got has a large
229			-- propagation delay. Thus, do not depend cpu_rdy and int_got.
230			-- This single entry FIFO stores a valid request if cpu_req_r = '1',
231			-- otherwise it is empty.
232			process(clk)
233			begin
234				if rising_edge(clk) then
235					-- store new request only if FIFO is empty
236					case to_x01(cpu_req_r) is
237						when '1' => null; -- FIFO is full
238						when '0' =>
239							cpu_write_r <= cpu_write;
240							cpu_addr_r  <= cpu_addr;
241							cpu_wdata_r <= cpu_wdata;
242							cpu_wmask_r <= cpu_wmask;
243	
244						when others => -- just for simulation
245							cpu_write_r <= 'X';
246							cpu_addr_r  <= (others => 'X');
247							cpu_wdata_r <= (others => 'X');
248							cpu_wmask_r <= (others => 'X');
249					end case;
250	
251					-- FIFO state logic
252					case to_x01(rst) is
253						when '1' =>    cpu_req_r <= '0';
254						when '0' =>	   cpu_req_r <=
255														 (cpu_req_r and not int_got) or -- keep if not yet acknowledged
256														 (not cpu_req_r and cpu_req);   -- or new request when empty
257						when others => cpu_req_r <= 'X';
258					end case;
259				end if;
260			end process;
261	
262			cpu_rdy   <= not cpu_req_r; -- ready when empty
263	
264			int_req   <= cpu_req_r;
265			int_write <= cpu_write_r;
266			int_addr  <= cpu_addr_r;
267			int_wdata <= cpu_wdata_r;
268			int_wmask <= cpu_wmask_r;
269		end generate g1;
270	
271	
272		------------------------------------------------------------------------------
273		g2: if OUTSTANDING_REQ = 2 generate
274			constant D_BITS : positive := CPU_DATA_BITS/8+CPU_DATA_BITS+CPU_ADDR_BITS+1;
275	
276	    signal put   : std_logic;
277	    signal din   : std_logic_vector(D_BITS-1 downto 0);
278	    signal full  : std_logic;
279	    signal valid : std_logic;
280	    signal dout  : std_logic_vector(D_BITS-1 downto 0);
281		begin
282			req_fifo: entity work.fifo_glue
283				generic map (
284					D_BITS => D_BITS)
285				port map (
286					clk => clk,
287					rst => rst,
288					put => put,
289					di  => din,
290					ful => full,
291					vld => valid,
292					do  => dout,
293					got => int_got);
294	
295			din <= cpu_wmask & cpu_wdata & std_logic_vector(cpu_addr) & (0 downto 0 => cpu_write);
296			put <= cpu_req;
297	
298			int_req   <= valid;
299			int_write <= dout(0);
300			int_addr  <= unsigned(dout(CPU_ADDR_BITS+1-1 downto 1));
301			int_wdata <= dout(CPU_DATA_BITS+CPU_ADDR_BITS+1-1 downto CPU_ADDR_BITS+1);
302			int_wmask <= dout(CPU_DATA_BITS/8+CPU_DATA_BITS+CPU_ADDR_BITS+1-1 downto CPU_DATA_BITS+CPU_ADDR_BITS+1);
303	
304			cpu_rdy <= not full;
305		end generate g2;
306	
307	
308		------------------------------------------------------------------------------
309		gt2: if OUTSTANDING_REQ > 2 generate
310			constant D_BITS : positive := CPU_DATA_BITS/8+CPU_DATA_BITS+CPU_ADDR_BITS+1;
311	
312	    signal put   : std_logic;
313	    signal din   : std_logic_vector(D_BITS-1 downto 0);
314	    signal full  : std_logic;
315	    signal valid : std_logic;
316	    signal dout  : std_logic_vector(D_BITS-1 downto 0);
317		begin
318			req_fifo: entity work.fifo_cc_got
319	      generic map (
320	        D_BITS         => D_BITS,
321	        MIN_DEPTH      => OUTSTANDING_REQ,
322	        DATA_REG       => false, -- otherwise OUTPUT_REG has no effect
323	        STATE_REG      => true,
324	        OUTPUT_REG     => true) -- extra register required for optimal synthesis on Altera FPGAs
325	      port map (
326	        rst       => rst,
327	        clk       => clk,
328	        put       => put,
329	        din       => din,
330	        full      => full,
331	        estate_wr => open,
332	        got       => int_got,
333	        dout      => dout,
334	        valid     => valid,
335	        fstate_rd => open);
336	
337			din <= cpu_wmask & cpu_wdata & std_logic_vector(cpu_addr) & (0 downto 0 => cpu_write);
338			put <= cpu_req;
339	
340			int_req   <= valid;
341			int_write <= dout(0);
342			int_addr  <= unsigned(dout(CPU_ADDR_BITS+1-1 downto 1));
343			int_wdata <= dout(CPU_DATA_BITS+CPU_ADDR_BITS+1-1 downto CPU_ADDR_BITS+1);
344			int_wmask <= dout(CPU_DATA_BITS/8+CPU_DATA_BITS+CPU_ADDR_BITS+1-1 downto CPU_DATA_BITS+CPU_ADDR_BITS+1);
345	
346			cpu_rdy <= not full;
347		end generate gt2;
348	end architecture rtl;
349

4	-- =============================================================================
5	-- Authors:					Patrick Lehmann
6	-- 									Martin Zabel
7	--
8	-- Entity:					Wrap different cache replacement policies.
9	--
10	-- Description:
11	-- -------------------------------------
12	--
13	-- **Supported policies:**
14	--
15	-- +----------+-----------------------+-----------+
16	-- | Abbr.    | Policies              | supported |
17	-- +==========+=======================+===========+
18	-- | RR       | round robin           | not yet   |
19	-- +----------+-----------------------+-----------+

21	-- +----------+-----------------------+-----------+

23	-- +----------+-----------------------+-----------+

25	-- +----------+-----------------------+-----------+
26	-- | LFU      | least frequently used | not yet   |
27	-- +----------+-----------------------+-----------+
28	--
29	-- **Command thruth table:**
30	--
31	-- +-----------+-----------+-------------+---------+-----------------------------------------------------+
32	-- | TagAccess | ReadWrite | Invalidate  | Replace | Command                                             |
33	-- +===========+===========+=============+=========+=====================================================+
34	-- |  0        |           |             |    0    | None                                                |
35	-- +-----------+-----------+-------------+---------+-----------------------------------------------------+
36	-- |  1        |    0      |    0        |    0    | TagHit and reading a cache line                     |
37	-- +-----------+-----------+-------------+---------+-----------------------------------------------------+
38	-- |  1        |    1      |    0        |    0    | TagHit and writing a cache line                     |
39	-- +-----------+-----------+-------------+---------+-----------------------------------------------------+
40	-- |  1        |    0      |    1        |    0    | TagHit and invalidate a  cache line (while reading) |
41	-- +-----------+-----------+-------------+---------+-----------------------------------------------------+
42	-- |  1        |    1      |    1        |    0    | TagHit and invalidate a  cache line (while writing) |
43	-- +-----------+-----------+-------------+---------+-----------------------------------------------------+
44	-- |  0        |           |    0        |    1    | Replace cache line                                  |
45	-- +-----------+-----------+-------------+---------+-----------------------------------------------------+
46	--
47	-- In a set-associative cache, each cache-set has its own instance of this component.
48	--
49	-- The input ``HitWay`` specifies the accessed way in a fully-associative or
50	-- set-associative cache.
51	--
52	-- The output ``ReplaceWay`` identifies the way which will be replaced as next by
53	-- a replace command. In a set-associative cache, this is the way in a specific
54	-- cache set (see above).
55	--

59	--										 Chair of VLSI-Design, Diagnostics and Architecture

69	-- WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
70	-- See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
71	-- limitations under the License.
72	-- =============================================================================
73	
74	library IEEE;
75	use IEEE.STD_LOGIC_1164.all;
76	use IEEE.NUMERIC_STD.all;
77	
78	library PoC;
79	use PoC.config.all;
80	use PoC.utils.all;
81	use PoC.vectors.all;
82	use PoC.strings.all;
83	
84	
85	entity cache_replacement_policy is
86		generic (
87			REPLACEMENT_POLICY : string		:= "LRU";
88			CACHE_WAYS				 : positive := 32
89		);
90		port (
91			Clock : in std_logic;
92			Reset : in std_logic;
93	
94			-- replacement interface
95			Replace		 : in	 std_logic;
96			ReplaceWay : out std_logic_vector(log2ceilnz(CACHE_WAYS) - 1 downto 0);
97	
98			-- cacheline usage update interface
99			TagAccess	 : in std_logic;
100			ReadWrite	 : in std_logic;
101			Invalidate : in std_logic;
102			HitWay		 : in std_logic_vector(log2ceilnz(CACHE_WAYS) - 1 downto 0)
103		);
104	end entity;
105	
106	
107	architecture rtl of cache_replacement_policy is
108		attribute KEEP				 : boolean;
109		attribute FSM_ENCODING : string;
110	
111		constant KEY_BITS : positive := log2ceilnz(CACHE_WAYS);
112	
113	begin
114		assert (str_equal(REPLACEMENT_POLICY, "RR") or
115						str_equal(REPLACEMENT_POLICY, "LRU"))
116			report "Unsupported replacement strategy"
117			severity error;
118	
119	
120		-- ===========================================================================
121		-- policy: RR - round robin
122		-- ===========================================================================
123		genRR : if str_equal(REPLACEMENT_POLICY, "RR") generate
124			constant VALID_BIT : natural := 0;
125	
126			subtype T_OPTION_LINE is std_logic_vector(0 downto 0);
127			type T_OPTION_LINE_VECTOR is array (natural range <>) of T_OPTION_LINE;
128	
129			signal OptionMemory : T_OPTION_LINE_VECTOR(CACHE_WAYS - 1 downto 0) := (others => (
130				VALID_BIT																																			=> '0')
131																																							 );
132	
133			signal ValidHit		: std_logic;
134			signal Pointer_us : unsigned(log2ceilnz(CACHE_WAYS) - 1 downto 0) := (others => '0');
135	
136		begin
137	--		ValidHit		<= OptionMemory(to_integer(unsigned(HitWay)))(VALID_BIT);
138	--		IsValid			<= ValidHit;
139	--
140	--		process(Clock)
141	--		begin
142	--			if rising_edge(Clock) then
143	--				if (Reset = '1') then
144	--					for i in 0 to CACHE_WAYS - 1 loop
145	--						OptionMemory(I)(VALID_BIT)	<= '0';
146	--					end loop;
147	--				else
148	--					if (Insert = '1') then
149	--						OptionMemory(to_integer(Pointer_us))(VALID_BIT) <= '1';
150	--					end if;
151	--
152	--					if (Invalidate = '1') then
153	--						OptionMemory(to_integer(unsigned(HitWay)))(VALID_BIT)			<= '0';
154	--					end if;
155	--				end if;
156	--			end if;
157	--		end process;
158	--
159	--		Replace				<= Insert;
160	--		ReplaceWay		<= std_logic_vector(Pointer_us);
161	--
162	--		process(Clock)
163	--		begin
164	--			if rising_edge(Clock) then
165	--				if (Reset = '1') then
166	--					Pointer_us		<= (others => '0');
167	--				else
168	--					if (Insert = '1') then
169	--						Pointer_us	<= Pointer_us + 1;
170	--					end if;
171	--				end if;
172	--			end if;
173	--		end process;
174		end generate;
175	
176		-- ===========================================================================
177		-- policy: LRU - least recently used
178		-- ===========================================================================
179		genLRU : if str_equal(REPLACEMENT_POLICY, "LRU") generate
180			signal LRU_Insert			: std_logic;
181			signal LRU_Invalidate : std_logic;
182			signal KeyIn					: std_logic_vector(log2ceilnz(CACHE_WAYS) - 1 downto 0);
183			signal LRU_Key				: std_logic_vector(log2ceilnz(CACHE_WAYS) - 1 downto 0);
184	
185		begin
186			-- Command Decoding
187			LRU_Insert		 <= (TagAccess and not Invalidate) or Replace;
188			LRU_Invalidate <= TagAccess and Invalidate;
189	
190			KeyIn <= LRU_Key when Replace = '1' else HitWay;
191	
192			-- Output
193			ReplaceWay <= LRU_Key;
194	
195			LRU : entity PoC.sort_lru_cache
196				generic map (
197					ELEMENTS => CACHE_WAYS
198				)
199				port map (
200					Clock => Clock,
201					Reset => Reset,
202	
203					Insert => LRU_Insert,
204					Free	 => LRU_Invalidate,
205					KeyIn	 => KeyIn,
206	
207					KeyOut => LRU_Key
208				);
209		end generate;
210	end architecture;

1	-- EMACS settings: -*-  tab-width: 2; indent-tabs-mode: t -*-
2	-- vim: tabstop=2:shiftwidth=2:noexpandtab
3	-- kate: tab-width 2; replace-tabs off; indent-width 2;
4	-- =============================================================================
5	-- Authors:					Patrick Lehmann
6	--									Martin Zabel
7	--
8	-- Entity:					Tag-unit with fully-parallel compare of tag.
9	--
10	-- Description:
11	-- -------------------------------------
12	-- Tag-unit with fully-parallel compare of tag.
13	--
14	-- Configuration
15	-- *************
16	--
17	-- +--------------------+----------------------------------------------------+
18	-- | Parameter          | Description                                        |
19	-- +====================+====================================================+
20	-- | REPLACEMENT_POLICY | Replacement policy. For supported policies see     |
21	-- |                    | PoC.cache_replacement_policy.                      |
22	-- +--------------------+----------------------------------------------------+
23	-- | CACHE_LINES        | Number of cache lines.                             |
24	-- +--------------------+----------------------------------------------------+
25	-- | ASSOCIATIVITY      | Associativity of the cache.                        |
26	-- +--------------------+----------------------------------------------------+
27	-- | ADDRESS_BITS       | Number of address bits. Each address identifies    |
28	-- |                    | exactly one cache line in memory.                  |
29	-- +--------------------+----------------------------------------------------+
30	--
31	--
32	-- Command truth table
33	-- *******************
34	--
35	-- +---------+-----------+-------------+---------+----------------------------------+
36	-- | Request | ReadWrite | Invalidate  | Replace | Command                          |
37	-- +=========+===========+=============+=========+==================================+
38	-- |   0     |    0      |    0        |    0    | None                             |
39	-- +---------+-----------+-------------+---------+----------------------------------+
40	-- |   1     |    0      |    0        |    0    | Read cache line                  |
41	-- +---------+-----------+-------------+---------+----------------------------------+
42	-- |   1     |    1      |    0        |    0    | Update cache line                |
43	-- +---------+-----------+-------------+---------+----------------------------------+
44	-- |   1     |    0      |    1        |    0    | Read cache line and discard it   |
45	-- +---------+-----------+-------------+---------+----------------------------------+
46	-- |   1     |    1      |    1        |    0    | Write cache line and discard it  |
47	-- +---------+-----------+-------------+---------+----------------------------------+
48	-- |   0     |           |    0        |    1    | Replace cache line.              |
49	-- +---------+-----------+-------------+---------+----------------------------------+
50	--
51	--
52	-- Operation
53	-- *********
54	--
55	-- All inputs are synchronous to the rising-edge of the clock `clock`.
56	--
57	-- All commands use ``Address`` to lookup (request) or replace a cache line.
58	-- Each command is completed within one clock cycle.
59	--
60	-- Upon requests, the outputs ``CacheMiss`` and ``CacheHit`` indicate (high-active)
61	-- immediately (combinational) whether the ``Address`` is stored within the cache, or not.
62	-- But, the cache-line usage is updated at the rising-edge of the clock.
63	-- If hit, ``LineIndex`` specifies the cache line where to find the content.
64	--
65	-- The output ``ReplaceLineIndex`` indicates which cache line will be replaced as
66	-- next by a replace command. The output ``OldAddress`` specifies the old tag stored at this
67	-- index. The replace command will store the ``Address`` and update the cache-line
68	-- usage at the rising-edge of the clock.
69	--
70	-- For a direct-mapped cache, the number of ``CACHE_LINES`` must be a power of 2.
71	-- For a set-associative cache, the expression ``CACHE_LINES / ASSOCIATIVITY``
72	-- must be a power of 2.
73	--
74	-- .. NOTE::
75	--    The port ``NewAddress`` has been removed. Use ``Address`` instead as
76	--    described above.
77	--
78	--    If ``Address`` is fed from a register and an Altera FPGA is used, then
79	--    Quartus Map converts the tag memory from a memory with asynchronous read to a
80	--    memory with synchronous read by adding a pass-through logic. Quartus Map
81	--    reports warning 276020 which is intended.
82	--
83	-- .. WARNING::
84	--
85	--    If the design is synthesized with Xilinx ISE / XST, then the synthesis
86	--    option "Keep Hierarchy" must be set to SOFT or TRUE.
87	--
88	-- License:
89	-- =============================================================================
90	-- Copyright 2007-2016 Technische Universitaet Dresden - Germany
91	--										 Chair of VLSI-Design, Diagnostics and Architecture
92	--
93	-- Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
94	-- you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
95	-- You may obtain a copy of the License at
96	--
97	--		http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
98	--
99	-- Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
100	-- distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
101	-- WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
102	-- See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
103	-- limitations under the License.
104	-- =============================================================================
105	
106	library IEEE;
107	use			IEEE.STD_LOGIC_1164.all;
108	use			IEEE.NUMERIC_STD.all;
109	
110	library PoC;
111	use			PoC.utils.all;
112	use			PoC.vectors.all;
113	
114	entity cache_tagunit_par is
115		generic (
116			REPLACEMENT_POLICY : string		:= "LRU";
117			CACHE_LINES				 : positive := 32;
118			ASSOCIATIVITY			 : positive := 32;
119			ADDRESS_BITS			 : positive := 8
120		);
121		port (
122			Clock : in std_logic;
123			Reset : in std_logic;
124	
125			Replace					 : in	 std_logic;
126			ReplaceLineIndex : out std_logic_vector(log2ceilnz(CACHE_LINES) - 1 downto 0);
127			OldAddress			 : out std_logic_vector(ADDRESS_BITS - 1 downto 0);
128	
129			Request		 : in	 std_logic;
130			ReadWrite	 : in	 std_logic;
131			Invalidate : in	 std_logic;
132			Address		 : in	 std_logic_vector(ADDRESS_BITS - 1 downto 0);
133			LineIndex	 : out std_logic_vector(log2ceilnz(CACHE_LINES) - 1 downto 0);
134			TagHit		 : out std_logic;
135			TagMiss		 : out std_logic
136		);
137	end entity;
138	
139	architecture rtl of cache_tagunit_par is
140		attribute KEEP : boolean;
141	
142		constant SETS : positive := CACHE_LINES / ASSOCIATIVITY;
143	
144		-- Returns true if unsigned value contains metalogical values.
145		-- Similar function is_x(unsigned) is only shipped with VHDL'08.
146		function contains_x(value : unsigned) return boolean is
147		begin
148			-- Use pragma to get rid of meaningless Quartus warning 10325 "ignored
149			-- choice containing meta-values ..." in function is_x(std_logic_vector).
150			-- Synthesis tools which ignore this pragma should return false for is_x().
151			-- synthesis translate_off
152			return is_x(std_logic_vector(value));
153			-- synthesis translate_on
154			return false; -- no meta-values in hardware here
155		end function;
156	
157	begin
158		-- ===========================================================================
159		-- Full-Associative Cache
160		-- ===========================================================================
161		genFA : if CACHE_LINES = ASSOCIATIVITY generate
162			constant TAG_BITS		: positive := ADDRESS_BITS;
163			constant WAY_BITS 	: positive := log2ceilnz(ASSOCIATIVITY);
164	
165			subtype T_TAG_LINE is std_logic_vector(TAG_BITS - 1 downto 0);
166			type T_TAG_LINE_VECTOR is array (natural range <>) of T_TAG_LINE;
167	
168			signal TagHits : std_logic_vector(CACHE_LINES - 1 downto 0); -- includes Valid
169	
170			signal TagMemory		: T_TAG_LINE_VECTOR(CACHE_LINES - 1 downto 0);
171			signal ValidMemory : std_logic_vector(CACHE_LINES - 1 downto 0)			:= (others => '0');
172	
173			signal HitWay : unsigned(WAY_BITS - 1 downto 0);
174	
175			signal Policy_ReplaceWay : std_logic_vector(WAY_BITS - 1 downto 0);
176			signal ReplaceWay_us	 	 : unsigned(WAY_BITS - 1 downto 0);
177	
178			signal TagHit_i	 : std_logic; -- includes Valid and Request
179			signal TagMiss_i : std_logic; -- includes Valid and Request
180		begin
181	
182			-- generate comparators and convert hit-vector to binary index (cache line address)
183			-- use process, so that "onehot2bin" does not report false errors in
184			-- simulation due to delta-cycles updates
185			process(Address, TagMemory, ValidMemory)
186				variable hits : std_logic_vector(CACHE_LINES - 1 downto 0); -- includes Valid
187			begin
188				for i in 0 to CACHE_LINES - 1 loop
189					hits(i) := to_sl(TagMemory(i) = Address and ValidMemory(i) = '1');
190				end loop;
191	
192				TagHits <= hits;
193				HitWay	<= onehot2bin(hits, 0);
194			end process;
195	
196			process(Clock)
197			begin
198				if rising_edge(Clock) then
199					if (Replace = '1') then
200						TagMemory(to_integer(ReplaceWay_us))	 <= Address;
201					end if;
202	
203					for i in ValidMemory'range loop
204						if Reset = '1' then
205							ValidMemory(i) <= '0';
206						elsif (Replace = '1' and ReplaceWay_us = i) or
207							(Invalidate = '1' and TagHits(i) = '1')
208						then
209							ValidMemory(i) <= Replace; -- clear when Invalidate
210						end if;
211					end loop;
212				end if;
213			end process;
214	
215			-- hit/miss calculation
216			TagHit_i	<= slv_or(TagHits) and Request;
217			TagMiss_i <= not slv_or(TagHits) and Request;
218	
219			-- outputs
220			LineIndex <= std_logic_vector(HitWay);
221			TagHit		<= TagHit_i;
222			TagMiss		<= TagMiss_i;
223	
224			ReplaceWay_us		 <= unsigned(Policy_ReplaceWay);
225			ReplaceLineIndex <= Policy_ReplaceWay;
226			OldAddress			 <= (others => 'X') when contains_x(ReplaceWay_us) else
227													TagMemory(to_integer(ReplaceWay_us));
228	
229			-- replacement policy
230			Policy : entity PoC.cache_replacement_policy
231				generic map (
232					REPLACEMENT_POLICY => REPLACEMENT_POLICY,
233					CACHE_WAYS				 => ASSOCIATIVITY
234				)
235				port map (
236					Clock => Clock,
237					Reset => Reset,
238	
239					Replace		 => Replace,
240					ReplaceWay => Policy_ReplaceWay,
241	
242	        TagAccess  => TagHit_i,
243	        ReadWrite  => ReadWrite,
244	        Invalidate => Invalidate,
245	        HitWay     => std_logic_vector(HitWay)
246	      );
247	  end generate;
248	
249	  -- ===========================================================================
250	  -- Direct-Mapped Cache
251	  -- ===========================================================================
252	  genDM : if ASSOCIATIVITY = 1 generate
253	    -- Addresses are splitted into a tag part and an index part.
254	    constant INDEX_BITS : positive := log2ceilnz(CACHE_LINES);
255	    constant TAG_BITS   : positive := ADDRESS_BITS - INDEX_BITS;
256	
257			subtype T_TAG_LINE is std_logic_vector(TAG_BITS-1 downto 0);
258			type T_TAG_LINE_VECTOR is array(natural range <>) of T_TAG_LINE;
259	
260			signal Address_Tag			: T_TAG_LINE;
261			signal Address_Index		: unsigned(INDEX_BITS - 1 downto 0);
262	
263			signal DM_TagHit	  : std_logic; -- includes Valid
264	
265			signal TagMemory	 : T_TAG_LINE_VECTOR(CACHE_LINES-1 downto 0);
266			signal ValidMemory : std_logic_vector(CACHE_LINES-1 downto 0) := (others => '0');
267	
268			-- If Address is fed from a register, then:
269			--
270			--  * the TagMemory must be implemented as distributed RAM on Xilinx
271			--    FPGAs because only this RAM has the intended mixed-port
272			--    read-during-write behavior. But even if ``ram_style`` is set,
273			--    synthesis sometimes generates a wrong netlist if KEEP_HIERARCHY is
274			--    set to off (default).
275			--
276			--  * Mapping the TagMemory to block RAM is however possible on Altera
277			--    FPGAs because Quartus adds the neccessary bypass logic.
278			--
279			-- If Address is not fed from a register, then:
280			--
281			--  * distributed RAM will be used on Xilinx FPGAs anyway,
282			--  * LUTs and FFs are used on Altera FPGAs.
283			attribute ram_style              : string;  -- XST specific
284			attribute ram_style of TagMemory : signal is "distributed";
285	
286			signal TagHit_i	 : std_logic;
287			signal TagMiss_i : std_logic;
288	
289			signal Tag   : T_TAG_LINE; -- read tag from memory
290			signal Valid : std_logic;  -- read valid from memory
291	
292	  begin
293			assert CACHE_LINES = 2**INDEX_BITS report "Unsupported number of cache lines." severity failure;
294	
295	    -- Split incoming 'Address'
296	    Address_Tag      <= Address(Address'left downto INDEX_BITS);
297	    Address_Index    <= unsigned(Address(INDEX_BITS-1 downto 0));
298	
299			-- Access tag / valid memory and compare tags.
300			Tag   <= (others => 'X') when contains_x(Address_Index) else
301						   TagMemory  (to_integer(Address_Index));
302			Valid <= 'X' when contains_x(Address_Index) else
303							 ValidMemory(to_integer(Address_Index));
304			DM_TagHit <= to_sl(Tag = Address_Tag) and Valid;
305	
306			process(Clock)
307			begin
308				if rising_edge(Clock) then
309					if (Replace = '1') then
310						TagMemory(to_integer(Address_Index)) <= Address_Tag;
311					end if;
312	
313					if Reset = '1' then
314						ValidMemory <= (others => '0');
315					elsif (Replace = '1') or (TagHit_i = '1' and Invalidate = '1')	then
316						ValidMemory(to_integer(Address_Index)) <= Replace; -- clear when Invalidate
317					end if;
318				end if;
319			end process;
320	
321			-- hit/miss calculation
322			TagHit_i	<= DM_TagHit and Request;
323			TagMiss_i <= not DM_TagHit and Request;
324	
325			-- outputs
326			LineIndex <= std_logic_vector(Address_Index);
327			TagHit		<= TagHit_i;
328			TagMiss		<= TagMiss_i;
329	
330			ReplaceLineIndex <= std_logic_vector(Address_Index);
331			OldAddress			 <= Tag & std_logic_vector(Address_Index);
332		end generate;
333	
334		-- ===========================================================================
335		-- Set-Assoziative Cache
336		-- ===========================================================================
337		genSA : if (ASSOCIATIVITY > 1) and (SETS > 1) generate
338	    -- Addresses are splitted into a tag part and an index part.
339			constant CACHE_SETS : positive := CACHE_LINES / ASSOCIATIVITY;
340	    constant INDEX_BITS : positive := log2ceilnz(CACHE_SETS);
341	    constant TAG_BITS   : positive := ADDRESS_BITS - INDEX_BITS;
342			constant WAY_BITS 	: positive := log2ceilnz(ASSOCIATIVITY);
343	
344			subtype T_TAG_LINE is std_logic_vector(TAG_BITS-1 downto 0);
345			type T_TAG_LINE_VECTOR is array(natural range <>) of T_TAG_LINE;
346	
347			type T_WAY_VECTOR is array(natural range<>) of std_logic_vector(WAY_BITS-1 downto 0);
348	
349			-- Splitted address
350			signal Address_Tag			: T_TAG_LINE;
351			signal Address_Index		: unsigned(INDEX_BITS - 1 downto 0);
352	
353			-- Way-specific signals
354			signal TagHits : std_logic_vector(ASSOCIATIVITY-1 downto 0); -- includes Valid
355			signal OldTags : T_TAG_LINE_VECTOR(ASSOCIATIVITY-1 downto 0);
356	
357			-- Cache-set specific signals
358			signal CS_TagAccess	 : std_logic_vector(CACHE_SETS-1 downto 0);
359			signal CS_Invalidate : std_logic_vector(CACHE_SETS-1 downto 0);
360			signal CS_Replace		 : std_logic_vector(CACHE_SETS-1 downto 0);
361			signal Policy_ReplaceWay : T_WAY_VECTOR(CACHE_SETS-1 downto 0);
362	
363			-- Way where hit occurs and way to replace
364			signal HitWay			: unsigned(WAY_BITS-1 downto 0);
365			signal ReplaceWay : unsigned(WAY_BITS-1 downto 0);
366	
367			signal TagHit_i	 : std_logic;
368			signal TagMiss_i : std_logic;
369	
370		begin
371	
372			assert CACHE_SETS = 2**INDEX_BITS report "Unsupported number of cache-sets." severity failure;
373	
374			----------------------------------------------------------------------------
375	    -- Split incoming 'Address'
376			-- Enable only one cache-set
377			----------------------------------------------------------------------------
378	    Address_Tag      <= Address(Address'left downto INDEX_BITS);
379	    Address_Index    <= unsigned(Address(INDEX_BITS-1 downto 0));
380	
381			----------------------------------------------------------------------------
382			-- Generate tag-memory and comparators for each way
383			----------------------------------------------------------------------------
384	
385			genWay : for way in 0 to ASSOCIATIVITY-1 generate
386				signal TagMemory	 : T_TAG_LINE_VECTOR(CACHE_SETS-1 downto 0);
387				signal ValidMemory : std_logic_vector(CACHE_SETS-1 downto 0) := (others => '0');
388	
389				-- If Address is fed from a register, then:
390				--
391				--  * the TagMemory must be implemented as distributed RAM on Xilinx
392				--    FPGAs because only this RAM has the intended mixed-port
393				--    read-during-write behavior. But even if ``ram_style`` is set,
394				--    synthesis sometimes generates a wrong netlist if KEEP_HIERARCHY is
395				--    set to off (default).
396				--
397				--  * Mapping the TagMemory to block RAM is however possible on Altera
398				--    FPGAs because Quartus adds the neccessary bypass logic.
399				--
400				-- If Address is not fed from a register, then:
401				--
402				--  * distributed RAM will be used on Xilinx FPGAs anyway,
403				--  * LUTs and FFs are used on Altera FPGAs.
404				attribute ram_style              : string;  -- XST specific
405				attribute ram_style of TagMemory : signal is "distributed";
406	
407				signal Tag   : T_TAG_LINE; -- read tag from memory
408				signal Valid : std_logic;  -- read valid from memory
409			begin
410				-- Access tag / valid memory and compare tags.
411				Tag   <= (others => 'X') when contains_x(Address_Index) else
412								 TagMemory  (to_integer(Address_Index));
413				Valid <= 'X' when contains_x(Address_Index) else
414								 ValidMemory(to_integer(Address_Index));
415	
416				TagHits(way) <= to_sl(Tag = Address_Tag) and Valid;
417	
418				-- memory update
419				process (Clock) is
420				begin  -- process
421					if rising_edge(Clock) then
422						if Replace = '1' and ReplaceWay = way then
423							TagMemory(to_integer(Address_Index)) <= Address_Tag;
424						end if;
425	
426						if Reset = '1' then
427							ValidMemory <= (others => '0');
428						elsif Replace = '1' and ReplaceWay = way then
429							ValidMemory(to_integer(Address_Index)) <= '1';
430						elsif Invalidate = '1' and TagHits(way) = '1' then
431							ValidMemory(to_integer(Address_Index)) <= '0';
432						end if;
433					end if;
434				end process;
435	
436				-- old address when replacing
437				OldTags(way) <= Tag;
438			end generate genWay;
439	
440			HitWay <= onehot2bin(TagHits, 0);
441	
442			----------------------------------------------------------------------------
443			-- Global hit / miss calculation and output
444			----------------------------------------------------------------------------
445			TagHit_i	<= slv_or(TagHits) and Request;
446			TagMiss_i <= not slv_or(TagHits) and Request;
447	
448			LineIndex <= std_logic_vector(HitWay) & std_logic_vector(Address_Index);
449			TagHit		<= TagHit_i;
450			TagMiss		<= TagMiss_i;
451	
452			----------------------------------------------------------------------------
453			-- Generate policy for each cache-set
454			----------------------------------------------------------------------------
455			process(Address_Index, TagHit_i)
456			begin
457				CS_TagAccess														 <= (others => '0');
458				if contains_x(Address_Index) then -- for simulation only
459					null;--TODO: CS_TagAccess <= (others => 'X');
460				else
461					CS_TagAccess(to_integer(Address_Index)) <= TagHit_i;
462				end if;
463			end process;
464	
465			process(Address_Index, Invalidate)
466			begin
467				CS_Invalidate														 <= (others => '0');
468				if contains_x(Address_Index) then -- for simulation only
469					null;--TODO: CS_Invalidate <= (others => 'X');
470				else
471					CS_Invalidate(to_integer(Address_Index)) <= Invalidate;
472				end if;
473			end process;
474	
475			process(Address_Index, Replace)
476			begin
477				CS_Replace															 <= (others => '0');
478				if contains_x(Address_Index) then -- for simulation only
479					null;--TODO: CS_Replace <= (others => 'X');
480				else
481					CS_Replace(to_integer(Address_Index)) <= Replace;
482				end if;
483			end process;
484	
485			genSet : for cs in 0 to CACHE_SETS-1 generate
486			begin
487				Policy : entity PoC.cache_replacement_policy
488					generic map (
489						REPLACEMENT_POLICY => REPLACEMENT_POLICY,
490						CACHE_WAYS				 => ASSOCIATIVITY
491					)
492					port map (
493						Clock => Clock,
494						Reset => Reset,
495	
496						Replace		 => CS_Replace(cs),
497						ReplaceWay => Policy_ReplaceWay(cs), -- way to replace
498	
499						TagAccess	 => CS_TagAccess(cs),
500						ReadWrite	 => ReadWrite,
501						Invalidate => CS_Invalidate(cs),
502						HitWay		 => std_logic_vector(HitWay) -- accessed way
503					);
504			end generate genSet;
505	
506			ReplaceWay <= (others => 'X') when contains_x(Address_Index) else
507										unsigned(Policy_ReplaceWay(to_integer(Address_Index)));
508	
509			----------------------------------------------------------------------------
510			-- Replace-specific outputs
511			----------------------------------------------------------------------------
512			ReplaceLineIndex <= std_logic_vector(ReplaceWay) & std_logic_vector(Address_Index);
513			OldAddress			 <= (others => 'X') when contains_x(ReplaceWay) else
514													OldTags(to_integer(ReplaceWay)) & std_logic_vector(Address_Index);
515	
516		end generate;
517	end architecture;
518

-- EMACS settings: -*-	tab-width: 2; indent-tabs-mode: t -*-
-- vim: tabstop=2:shiftwidth=2:noexpandtab
-- kate: tab-width 2; replace-tabs off; indent-width 2;
-- =============================================================================
-- Authors:         Patrick Lehmann
--                  Martin Zabel
--
-- Entity:          Optimized LRU list implementation for Caches.
--
-- Description:
-- -------------------------------------
-- This is an optimized implementation of ``sort_lru_list`` to be used for caches.
-- Only keys are stored within this list, and these keys are the index of the
-- cache lines. The list initially contains all indizes from 0 to ELEMENTS-1.
-- The least-recently used index ``KeyOut`` is always valid.
--
-- The first outputed least-recently used index will be ELEMENTS-1.
--
-- The inputs ``Insert``, ``Free``, ``KeyIn``, and ``Reset`` are synchronous to the
-- rising-edge of the clock ``clock``. All control signals are high-active.
--
-- Supported operations:
--  * **Insert:** Mark index ``KeyIn`` as recently used, e.g., when a cache-line
--    was accessed.
--  * **Free:** Mark index ``KeyIn`` as least-recently used. Apply this operation,
--    when a cache-line gets invalidated.
--
-- License:
-- =============================================================================
-- Copyright 2007-2016 Technische Universitaet Dresden - Germany
--										 Chair of VLSI-Design, Diagnostics and Architecture
